intro: I am interested in the remote Scientist III, Data Engineer role with Thermo Fisher Scientific. My background aligns strongly with AWS data engineering, PySpark, SQL, S3, Glue, Redshift, Athena, ETL/data solutions, performance tuning, production support, and regulated enterprise delivery.
body: At Citi, I built automated Python and Pandas ETL pipelines that ingested and standardized telemetry from 6,000+ enterprise endpoints. I also designed AWS-oriented data platform patterns using S3 landing zones, Glue-based ETL, Athena-style lake access, and Redshift analytics to support forecasting, reporting, and operational decision-making.

Your role's focus on PySpark/EMR-style processing, AWS Athena, S3, Redshift, Glue, SQL optimization, APIs, data lake patterns, CI/CD, governance, security, production support, and cross-functional delivery maps well to my strongest recent experience. I have built validation routines, optimized SQL and storage patterns, containerized Python ETL jobs, and supported production troubleshooting in regulated enterprise environments.

I would bring senior data engineering discipline to Thermo Fisher's data platform work: automate manual processes, produce clean and reliable data, improve scalability, document transformations, monitor failures, and collaborate with scientific, laboratory, business, and technical stakeholders. I am actively building deeper Databricks, Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Terraform, Jenkins, and streaming fluency, while my strongest production base is AWS, Python, SQL, PySpark, Redshift, Glue, data quality, and operational reliability.
conclusion: I would welcome the opportunity to help Thermo Fisher Scientific deliver scalable, secure, and reliable data platform solutions that support scientific and business outcomes.
